predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, SpaceX will publicly file an IPO registration statement (S-1 or F-1) on SEC EDGAR for a U.S. listing.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolve YES if an S-1/F-1 registration statement for Space Exploration Technologies Corp. (SpaceX) is publicly available on SEC EDGAR on or before the deadline; otherwise NO.
evidence: Recent reporting indicates SpaceX confidentially filed for an IPO, setting up a potential record offering: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/01/spacex-confidentially-files-for-ipo-setting-stage-for-record-offering.html
monitoring_signals: SEC EDGAR new filings search for SpaceX / Space Exploration Technologies
Exchange (NYSE/Nasdaq) listing announcements and underwriting bank press releases
Secondary-market pricing (Forge/EquityZen) and SpaceX tender offer notices
predictions.rationale: Confidential filings often precede a public S-1 within months, but timing is sensitive to market volatility, geopolitical risk, and readiness of audited disclosures.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, the IAEA Board of Governors will adopt a resolution that formally censures Iran for non-compliance (or failure to cooperate) with safeguards obligations.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolve YES if an IAEA Board of Governors meeting outcome includes an adopted resolution (IAEA press release/official document) that explicitly condemns/censures Iran for non-compliance or lack of cooperation with safeguards; otherwise NO.
evidence: Escalating U.S.-Iran conflict and heightened diplomatic strain increase likelihood of formal multilateral action: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/01/us/politics/trump-iran-war.html
monitoring_signals: IAEA BoG meeting agendas (March/June cycles) and Director General reports
EU3/U.S. draft resolution reporting (Reuters/AP) and IAEA press releases
Iranian Atomic Energy Organization/Foreign Ministry responses
predictions.rationale: In periods of heightened confrontation, the Board more often turns to formal resolutions to increase pressure, though consensus politics and member-state bargaining add uncertainty.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, NASA will publish an official Artemis program schedule update that sets Artemis III (first crewed lunar landing of the Artemis program) as NET 2028 or later.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolve YES if NASA (e.g., NASA.gov press release, official Artemis pages, or a NASA program update) states Artemis III is “NET 2028” or later; otherwise NO.
evidence: Artemis II launched and mission details highlight engineering and schedule sensitivity: https://www.sciencenews.org/article/nasa-artemis-launch-humans-moon
monitoring_signals: NASA Artemis timeline page updates and Administrator briefings
GAO/NASA OIG reports on Artemis/SLS/Orion/HLS readiness
Starship HLS milestones and contract modification notices
predictions.rationale: Complex integration (HLS readiness, suits, landing systems) historically drives rightward schedule moves; a formal update pushing Artemis III beyond 2027 is more likely than on-time landing.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, TrendForce (or an equivalent major memory-market analyst) will report that average PC DRAM contract prices are up at least 20% year-over-year in a monthly or quarterly update.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolve YES if a TrendForce report (or comparable analyst update explicitly presenting YoY PC DRAM contract pricing) states YoY change is ≥ +20% at any point on/before the deadline; otherwise NO.
evidence: Recent commentary notes surging memory prices and hardware BOM pressure: https://www.jeffgeerling.com/blog/2026/dram-pricing-is-killing-the-hobbyist-sbc-market/
monitoring_signals: TrendForce DRAM contract price monthly/quarterly reports
DRAMeXchange spot vs contract divergence and OEM commentary
Samsung/SK hynix/Micron earnings and supply guidance
predictions.rationale: Memory cycles can move quickly; a 20% YoY jump is plausible if supply remains tight and AI/HBM capacity crowds out commodity DRAM, but contract pricing is stickier than spot.

predictions.text: By 2026-08-31, U.S. state WARN disclosures will show at least 10,000 total planned layoffs at Oracle (sum across all Oracle-related WARN notices dated in 2026).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolve YES if aggregating publicly posted WARN notices across U.S. states for 2026 (by notice date) yields ≥10,000 affected workers where the employer is Oracle/Oracle America/Oracle Cloud or clearly Oracle-branded subsidiaries; otherwise NO.
evidence: Reporting describes large Oracle layoffs and cost-cutting behavior: https://www.marketwatch.com/story/fired-via-email-some-of-the-30-000-workers-cut-by-oracle-woke-up-to-a-morning-message-saying-they-were-laid-off-89a7af94
monitoring_signals: California/NY/TX/WA WARN databases for Oracle entries
Oracle headcount/disclosures in quarterly filings and earnings calls
Local media reports of site-level reductions
predictions.rationale: WARN captures only some layoffs and thresholds vary by structure, but sustained reductions across multiple sites could plausibly exceed 10k in disclosed planned cuts by late summer.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, Google will announce that stable-channel Chrome enables a post-quantum (or hybrid post-quantum) TLS key exchange by default for at least a majority of connections (≥50%) on supported platforms.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolve YES if Google/Chrome (blog post, release notes, or Chromium announcement) states that PQC or hybrid PQC key exchange is enabled by default in stable Chrome and is deployed to ≥50% of eligible TLS connections (or equivalent majority wording) by the deadline; otherwise NO.
evidence: Recent coverage highlights growing concern that fewer qubits may threaten widely used encryption, increasing migration urgency: https://www.sciencenews.org/article/quantum-bits-crack-internet-encryption
monitoring_signals: Chrome Security/Chromium blog posts mentioning Kyber/ML-KEM or hybrid TLS
IETF TLS WG updates and major CDN deployment notes (Cloudflare/Akamai/Fastly)
Telemetry or rollout percentage statements in Chrome release communications
predictions.rationale: Chrome has incentives to lead PQC migration, but compatibility and ecosystem readiness constrain a majority-by-default rollout timeline.

predictions.text: By 2026-09-30, at least one major European NATO member (Germany, France, UK, Italy, Poland, Spain, or the Netherlands) will publish an official budget plan increasing annual defense spending by at least €10 billion (or £8.5 billion for the UK) versus its 2025 enacted level, explicitly citing U.S./NATO commitment uncertainty.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolve YES if an official government budget document/press release or parliamentary budget submission specifies an annual defense outlay increase meeting the threshold and explicitly references U.S. policy uncertainty, NATO reliability, or U.S. withdrawal threats as a motivating factor; otherwise NO.
evidence: Trump publicly floated leaving NATO, increasing alliance uncertainty: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/01/trump-says-hes-considering-pulling-us-out-of-paper-tiger-nato.html
monitoring_signals: European budget releases and defense white papers
NATO summit communiqués and national statements on burden-sharing
Major procurement announcements tied to higher toplines
predictions.rationale: European rearmament is already underway; explicit linkage to U.S./NATO uncertainty is the gating factor, but political incentives make public justification increasingly likely.

predictions.text: By 2026-10-31, at least one of CVS Caremark, Express Scripts, or Optum Rx will publish a formulary update indicating coverage (preferred or non-preferred) for Eli Lilly’s newly approved oral GLP-1 obesity pill for the 2027 plan year.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolve YES if a publicly accessible PBM formulary document, press release, or official payer/PBM update states the product is covered for obesity/weight management (not only diabetes) for 2027 plan designs by the deadline; otherwise NO.
evidence: FDA approval of Eli Lilly’s GLP-1 weight-loss pill expands competitive and payer dynamics: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/01/eli-lilly-glp-1-pill-foundayo-approved-for-obesity.html
monitoring_signals: PBM formulary preview releases (typically mid-year through fall)
Lilly earnings commentary on access, rebates, and payer wins
Large insurer medical-policy updates referencing the oral GLP-1
predictions.rationale: PBMs are pressured on obesity-drug spend but competition and oral convenience support at least some coverage inclusion by the next major plan-year cycle.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, the University of Michigan 1-year-ahead inflation expectations (final reading) will be at or above 5.0% in at least one monthly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolve YES if the U. Michigan Surveys of Consumers reports 1-year inflation expectations ≥5.0% in any final monthly release dated on/before the deadline; otherwise NO.
evidence: Oil price spike tied to Iran conflict increases near-term inflation salience: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/02/oil-prices-today-wti-brent-trump-speech-iran-war-.html
monitoring_signals: Preliminary and final Michigan survey releases
Retail gasoline moves and headline CPI surprises
Inflation expectations in NY Fed survey and market breakevens
predictions.rationale: Energy shocks can push survey expectations higher, but anchoring and rapid mean reversion are common; hitting 5% is plausible yet still a tail event over a short window.
